title: Water Inlet Problem
shortDescription: Insufficient water supply to the washing machine.
fullDescription: Error E10 indicates the washer is not receiving enough water. Check supply taps, inlet hoses, and filter screens.

fixSteps.title: Reset the washer
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 5 minutes and restart. Many errors clear after a power reset.

fixSteps.title: Check the relevant component
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the component related to water inlet problem for obvious issues.

fixSteps.title: Test with multimeter
fixSteps.instruction: Use a multimeter to test the suspected component for proper electrical values.

fixSteps.title: Replace if faulty
fixSteps.instruction: Replace the faulty component and test the washer through a complete cycle.

whenToCallPro: If basic troubleshooting does not resolve the error, contact an AEG-certified service technician.

faq.q: What does AEG error E10 mean?
faq.a: Insufficient water supply to the washing machine. This is one of the most common AEG washer error codes.

faq.q: Can I use the washer with E10?
faq.a: The washer typically will not operate normally with this error. The fault should be addressed before continued use.

faq.q: How much does it cost to fix E10?
faq.a: DIY repairs range from 0 to 70 dollars. Professional repair typically costs 100 to 250 dollars.

title: Water Leak Detected
shortDescription: Water has been detected in the base pan of the machine.
fullDescription: Error E13 triggers the aqua-stop leak protection system. A float switch in the base detected standing water from an internal leak.

fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 5 minutes and restart. Many errors clear after a power reset.
